paper_title: APPLICATION OF STATCOMS TO IMPROVE STATIC VOLTAGE STABILITY FOR VIETNAM POWER SYSTEM WITH GRID CONNECTION OF LARGE NUCLEAR POWER PLANT

entities.description: The ability of a power system to maintain steady voltages at all buses in the system after being subjected to a disturbance.
The measure of the ability of interconnected electric systems to reliably move or transfer electric power from one area to another.
The management of reactive power to improve the performance of AC power systems.
Static Synchronous Compensators used to provide reactive power compensation and improve voltage stability.
A software tool used for power system transmission analysis and generation performance simulation.
A method used to analyze the relationship between real power (P) and voltage (V) to determine stability limits.
A digital simulation model of the Vietnamese national power grid including 1680 generators, 18 substations, 43 buses, and 78 transmission lines.
The load margin at which voltage collapse occurs in the base case.

entities.id: System load margin with STATCOMs (2131.25 MW)
entities.description: The significantly improved load margin after the installation of STATCOMs.
The lowest voltage value at the margin of stability limit for Da Nang bus.
The amount of increase in power transfer capability achieved by installing STATCOMs.

entities.id: Reactive power margin at Da Nang (157.07 MVAr)
entities.description: The reduced reactive power margin during specific branch outages.
The primary field of study regarding power systems and voltage stability.
The primary researcher of the study.

entities.id: College of Engineering Technology, Can Tho University
entities.description: The academic institution where the author is affiliated.
